plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-594 RP19.P05.M04.S08-RP19.P05.M06.S05 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-594 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP19.P05 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the thirteen remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first five rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H19), Claude review packet (C19), and CP00-595 handoff, without opening client portal, secure link, client review, or watermark runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-594 is the 12th pack in the RP19 client-portal descriptor-only series (CP583-CP594) and is internally consistent across every reviewed artifact. Identity/scope (pack_id CP00-594, risk B, 40 units, range RP19.P05.M04.S08-RP19.P05.M06.S05, upstream CP00-593, next CP00-595/RP19.P05.M06.S06) matches the prompt baseline, review-baseline.json, manifest.json, the live closeout-pack-plan.json entry (matching source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65..., deviation_from_plan=false), the registry CP594 pack binding, and the embedded contract p05_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor projection. Distribution reconciles to 40 in every source: deliverables implementation14/security_audit4/fixture13/test6/hermes_evidence2/claude_review1; micro sections RP19.P05.M04 "Secondary Workflow Slice"=13, RP19.P05.M05 "Permission And Audit Binding"=22, RP19.P05.M06 "Synthetic Fixture Set"=5. Implementation (registry.js/service.js/validators.js) follows the established pattern: freezeCp594Result asserts every no-write/no-real-data flag false (no client portal/secure link/watermark/review runtime, no state writes, no DB rows, no object storage, no Hermes runtime receipt, no command/UI/test-path runtime, no real fixture data, no permission bypass, no leak), and validateClientPortalCoreCp594P05WorkflowPermissionSliceDescriptor enforces descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false on all 40 rows; the inherited ROW_EXTRAS chain resolves to the CP583 base of pure descriptor metadata with no real data. All 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js; model.test.js and validate-rp19-client-portal-core-contract.mjs deepEqual the contract against registry constants (gating drift) and assert coverage/slice/handoff; command-evidence.json reports 27 build/validate/test commands at exit 0. Authority boundary is intact: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; Hermes appears only as a descriptor_only no_write receipt shape (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ review hardening holds: the readiness auth-preflight passed cleanly (exit 0, 1446 stdout bytes, well-formed success JSON, not fenced/tool-call-shaped/malformed), and forbidden_review_evidence enumerates exactly the required rejection set. The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly reported false (status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor); production_ready_candidate=true is a local descriptor-verification flag only and does not overclaim or weaken gates. The lone surface quirk (the pack's own untracked docs appearing in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved) is by-design tooling behavior with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty, matching CP593's already-closed scope file. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; this does not block pack or goal closeout, and human final approval still governs any enterprise-trust or runtime-opening claim.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-594 client portal core p05 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
